title: ElasticSearch Cluster Red Status Due to Unassigned Shards
symptoms: ElasticSearch cluster health showing RED status
Remaining data nodes showing disk utilization above 85% watermark
Search queries returning partial results with missing data
Log ingestion pipeline backing up with indexing rejections
root_cause: Data node disk failure combined with insufficient free disk space on remaining nodes preventing shard reallocation, caused by undersized cluster for the current data volume
resolution: Added two new data nodes with 2TB storage each, manually triggered shard reallocation, recovered data from replicas on healthy nodes, and replaced the failed disk on es-data-05
preventive_actions: Implement disk space capacity planning with 40% headroom for reallocation
Add disk watermark monitoring with alerts at 70% utilization
Deploy disk health monitoring (SMART) on all ElasticSearch data nodes
Implement ILM (Index Lifecycle Management) for automatic data tiering
Create cluster capacity right-sizing review quarterly

timeline.event: es-data-05 goes offline due to disk failure
Cluster attempts shard reallocation, disk watermark blocks it
Cluster enters RED status with 240 unassigned shards
P1 alert fires for cluster red status
Disk failure on es-data-05 confirmed, remaining nodes over watermark
Two new data nodes provisioned and joined to cluster
Shard reallocation initiated to new nodes
Cluster recovering, status changing from RED to YELLOW

embedding_summary: ElasticSearch cluster RED status from data node disk failure with remaining nodes unable to accept shard reallocation due to disk watermark exceeded. 240 unassigned shards across 12 indices for 1 hour. Search returning partial results. Resolved by adding data nodes and triggering shard reallocation.
